conversations.say: Maren, let's lock in our disclosure boundary. We must agree on a sentence describing collaborative labor that names no contributor before outsider pressure grows.
conversations.offer: Bounded worker testimony guidelines protecting anonymity
conversations.request: Acceptance of our proposed contributor protection boundary

conversations.say: Jules, if you claim future stewardship of R, what concrete protections and governance structures will you establish for our anonymous contributors?
conversations.offer: Insight on contributor rights and collective labor protection
conversations.request: A binding commitment to protect Night Desk laborers under any succession model

conversations.say: Frankie, celebrated bylines often erase collective labor. Let's examine how your early newsroom history contributed to that erasure without sacrificing our people.
conversations.offer: Early newsroom workflow context and routing slip insights
conversations.request: Your candid critique of individual authorship versus collective workflow

public_statement: Fifteen years of investigative reporting rests on the labor of many hands who remain anonymous. We reject any narrative that turns contributors into disposable myth.

commitments.promise: Establish a joint disclosure boundary regarding collective labor before speaking to outsiders

correction: Clarified that Night Desk workflow represents collaborative labor rather than a single authorial mantle, and reaffirmed that contributor schedules do not imply automated or singular authorship.
goal_progress: Challenged Jules on governance and contributor rights
Engaged Frankie on the tension between individual bylines and collective labor
reasoning_summary: Tessa advances her Section 2 objectives by cementing her protective boundary agreement with Maren, pressing Jules on governance structures for contributors, and interrogating Frankie's newsroom history regarding editorial erasure. Beliefs shift toward a human mantle as collaborative infrastructure is further substantiated.
